title: ranunculus ficaria var. grandiflorus
intro: Ranunculus ficaria var. grandiflorus, commonly known as lesser celandine, is a perennial plant in the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae) that is native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been found to have medicinal properties in treating various health issues. The health benefits of lesser celandine include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Lesser celandine can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or ointment, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
